subject: Chemistry
solution:
CCl4 is used as fire extinguishers under the name of pyrene.
The dense vapours of carbon tetrachloride forms a protective layer on the burning objects and avoids the oxygen or air to come in contact with the fire from the burning objects and provides incombustible vapours.

Chemical paint strippers that include methylene chloride and phenol have been extensively used to remove polymer coatings from metallic substrates. These strippers are inexpensive and remove polymeric organic coatings quickly and easily from a variety of metallic substrates without damage to the substrate.

Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane (DDT) is an organic halogen that acts by killing insects rapidly upon contact. DDT is harm to all biota of the planet as it is non-biodegradable, and tends to become more concentrated as it moves up the food chain.

The freons are simple fluorinated and aliphatic organic compounds that contain fluorine, carbon, hydrogen, chlorine, bromine etc. These are used as the refrigerants and propellants. Chlorofluorocarbons, Hydrochlorofluorocarbons are the types of freons. They have a low boiling point and low suface tension due to which they are used in refrigerators, air conditioners etc. However, they undergo photo-dissociation when exposed to the UV rays, releasing a reactive chlorine ion that is capable of reacting with ozone. This reaction is responsible for the depletion of the ozone in the stratosphere. Hence, the correct answer is 'Freon'.

Hence, CCl4 used in fire extinguisher. CH2Cl2 used as paint remover. Freons used in refrigerator and AC.DDT used as non Biodegradable insecticide.

solution:
Boron is extremely hard as it exists as B12 icosahedral molecular units which accounts for its high lattice energy. So, statement-I is correct.

Due to small atomic size boron forms strong covalent bonds with the neighbouring atoms. Thus, boron atoms are closely packed in its solid, thus boron has high melting and boiling points. So, statement-II is also correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Carbon atom have the tendency to link with one another through covalent bonds to form chains because of its tetra valency. This property is called catenation tendency. Carbon shows maximum catenation tendency among its group elements due to its high bond enthalpy. As we go down, the group size increases and electronegativity decreases therefore catenation tendency decreases.
 So,order of catenation tendency is 

C>Si>Ge≈Sn

subject: Chemistry
solution:
As bond length increases, bond energy decreases.

Bond Bond energy (kJ mol-1)
C - C 348
Si - Si 297
Ge - Ge 260
Sn - Sn 240

So, bond enthalpy order C - C > Si - Si > Ge - Ge > Sn - Sn

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The given reactions describe the Borax Bead Test.
When borax ($Na_2B_4O_7 \cdot 10H_2O$) is heated, it first loses water and then melts to form a transparent glassy bead consisting of sodium metaborate ($NaBO_2$) and boric anhydride ($B_2O_3$).
$Na_2B_4O_7 \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2NaBO_2 (X) + B_2O_3 (Y)$
In the non-luminous (oxidizing) flame, $B_2O_3$ reacts with $CuSO_4$ to form copper(II) metaborate ($Z$), which is blue in color.
$CuSO_4 + B_2O_3 \xrightarrow{Non-Luminous} Cu(BO_2)_2 (Z) + SO_3$
In $Cu(BO_2)_2$, the oxidation state of $Cu$ is $+2$.
In the luminous (reducing) flame, the copper(II) metaborate is reduced by carbon to copper(I) metaborate ($Q$), which is colorless/red.
$2Cu(BO_2)_2 (Z) + 2NaBO_2 (X) + C \xrightarrow{Luminous} 2CuBO_2 (Q) + Na_2B_4O_7 + CO$
In $CuBO_2$, the oxidation state of $Cu$ is $+1$.
Therefore, the oxidation states of $Cu$ in $Z$ and $Q$ are $+2$ and $+1$ respectively.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Reaction of ammonia with diborane gives initially B2H6.2NH3 which is formulated as BH2NH32+ BH4- ; further heating gives borazine, B3N3H6 known as ''inorganic benzene'' in view of its ring structure with alternate BH and NH groups.

3B2H6+6NH3→3BH2NH32+BH4-→Heat2B3N3H6+12H2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Analyzing statements about Group 13 elements (B, Al, Ga, In, Tl):
A. Ionic radii of M³⁺ cations: Down a group, more electron shells are added, so ionic radii increase, not decrease. FALSE
B. Electronegativity: Generally decreases down the group, though Ga shows an anomaly with higher electronegativity than Al. The statement is approximately true but has exceptions.
C. First ionization enthalpy: B (800.6 kJ/mol) is highest due to smallest size and highest nuclear charge density. Al (577.5), Ga (579), In (558), Tl (589). TRUE
D. Trichlorides and triodides: BCl₃, AlCl₃, GaCl₃, etc. are all covalent compounds (electron-deficient). BI₃, AlI₃, GaI₃, etc. are also covalent (I⁻ is large and polarizable). TRUE
The clearly correct statements are C and D.

solution: Silicones with medium length chains behave as viscous oils, jellies and greases. They are chemically inert, i.e., resistant to oxidation. Since silicones are surrounded by non-polar alkyl groups, hence, are hydrophobic in nature. Being biocompatible, they are used in surgical and cosmetic implants. Hence, all options are correct.

solution: For a good quality cement the ratio of silica (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SiO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>) and alumina (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Al</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>) should be between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>to</mi><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Similarly, the ratio of lime (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CaO</mi></math>) to the total oxide mixtures consisting of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SiO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>Al</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> should be roughly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, If lime is in excess, the cement cracks during setting.

solution:
Due to non-availability of d orbitals, boron is unable to expand its octet. Therefore, the maximum covalence of boron cannot exceed 4.

Boron is the first element from top in group 13 and the atomic size increases down the group. Therefore, boron is smallest in size.

Assertion A: True

Reason R: True but not correct explanation.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The strength of the acidic character of boron trihalides depends upon π-π back bonding. In boron trihalides, π-π -back bonding occurs due to empty orbital of boron and filled orbitals of halogens.

 

The π-π back bonding is shown maximum byBF3, as the size of B and F are small and comparatively same. Due to this effect tendency of accepting lone-pair of electrons of boron decreases as the size of halogen increases. The order of size of halogens are F < Cl < Br < I . Thus, acidic nature is in order BF3<BCl3<BBr3<Bl3.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I:
Aluminium reacts with aqueous sodium hydroxide to form sodium tetrahydroxoaluminate(III) and liberates hydrogen gas. The reaction is given by:
$2Al + 2NaOH + 6H_2O \rightarrow 2Na^+[Al(OH)_4]^- + 3H_2$
The complex ion formed is $[Al(OH)_4]^-$, not $[Al(OH)_6]^{3-}$. Thus, Statement I is false.

Statement II:
For $ICl_4^-$, the central iodine atom has $7$ valence electrons $+ 1$ (from negative charge) $= 8$ electrons. It forms $4$ sigma bonds with Cl atoms and has $2$ lone pairs. The hybridization is $sp^3d^2$ and the shape is square planar.
For $ClO_3^-$, the central chlorine atom has $7$ valence electrons $+ 1 = 8$ electrons. It forms $3$ sigma bonds with O atoms and has $1$ lone pair. The hybridization is $sp^3$ and the shape is trigonal pyramidal.
For $IBr_2^-$, the central iodine atom has $7$ valence electrons $+ 1 = 8$ electrons. It forms $2$ sigma bonds with Br atoms and has $3$ lone pairs. The hybridization is $sp^3d$ and the shape is linear.
Thus, Statement II is true.

Therefore, Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

Answer: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is true but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I,

SnO, PbO, SnO2, PbO2 : Amphoteric CO2, SiO2, GeO2, GeO : Acidic CO : Neutral

According to Statement II,
Carbon does not have d-orbitals so can not form pπ-dπ Bond with itself. Due to properties of catenation and pπ-pπ bond formation, carbon is able to show allotropic forms. Allotropic forms of carbon can be crystalline and amorphous.
Diamond and graphite are two well-known crystalline forms of carbon. 

Hence option C is the answer.

subject: Chemistry
options: All <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> angles are of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>120</mn><mo>°</mo></math>

The two <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> bonds (bridged and terminal) are not of same length.

Terminal <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> bonds have less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math> -character when compared to bridging bonds.
Its fragment, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>BH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo></math> behaves as a Lewis acid.
solution:
θ2>θ1,    ∴B-H (terminal) having less p
character as compare to bridge bond.

Both B-H-B (bridge bond and terminal bond) do not have same bond length.

B-H-B bond angle is ≈90°

BH3 is electron deficient  species and therefore acts as Lewis acid
